faqs.question: What are the signs I need a water softener?
faqs.answer: Common signs of hard water include white scale buildup on faucets and showerheads, dry skin and hair after showering, spots on dishes after washing, stiff laundry, and reduced water heater efficiency.

faqs.question: How does a water softener work?
faqs.answer: A water softener uses an ion exchange process to remove calcium and magnesium minerals from your water supply. Hard water passes through resin beads that swap the hard minerals for sodium ions, producing soft water throughout your home.

faqs.question: How much maintenance does a water softener require?
faqs.answer: Water softeners are relatively low-maintenance. You'll need to add salt to the brine tank every few weeks and have the system inspected annually. Our maintenance plans include regular check-ups to keep your system performing at its best.
